title: approvals — Eloquent observers
description: Five observers enforce the engine's write-time invariants: action-key format + registry integrity, template versioning + one-active constraint, instance status-machine, requirement quorum evaluation, and decision integrity (the security-critical one). Every observer refuses writes that violate its invariant AND fires the appropriate event or ApprovalSecurityViolation.

handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `aac_`
creating → validate action_key matches `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*)+$` (refused otherwise with `INVALID_ACTION_KEY_FORMAT`)
creating → validate namespace matches `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`
creating → derive action_class_fqcn is a real class implementing ApprovableActionInterface (integrity guard beyond the seeder)
creating → fire ApprovableActionRegistered
updating → refuse updates to action_key (immutable — action_key is the natural key for FK from approval_templates)
updating → refuse updates to action_class_fqcn while any approval_instances reference this action_key (breaking the executor's replay path)
updating (is_active flipped false) → warn if any active templates reference this action_key (does NOT refuse — the templates go dormant when the action is deactivated)
deleting → refuse hard-delete while any approval_templates reference this action_key (RESTRICT FK also enforces at DB level)
creating → populate ULID prefix `apt_`
creating → set version=1 always
creating → validate action_key resolves to an approvable_actions row with is_active=true (returns `PLATFORM_ACTION_NOT_ACTIVE` otherwise)
creating → enforce one-is-active invariant: if is_active=true, deactivate any peer row matching (tenant_id, application_id, action_key, name); dispatch ApprovalTemplateDeactivated for each peer
creating → consume 1 unit of approval_templates_max_active entitlement (throws `TENANT_TEMPLATE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` when cap reached)
creating → validate when_expression compiles via ExpressionLanguageAdapter::compile() (returns `INVALID_EXPRESSION` on parse error)
creating → fire ApprovalTemplateCreated

updating → intercept semantic-behaviour field changes (when_expression, on_timeout, allows_recall, expires_after_hours): PREVENT direct update — instead insert a NEW row with version+1, set is_active=true on the new row, set is_active=false on this row; fire ApprovalTemplateVersionBumped (design.md D-A4 — old versions preserved for audit)
updating → non-semantic-field changes (name, description, priority, reminder_config): allow direct update; fire ApprovalTemplateUpdated
updating (is_active flipped true) → deactivate peer + fire ApprovalTemplateActivated + ApprovalTemplateDeactivated
updating (is_active flipped false) → fire ApprovalTemplateDeactivated (trigger='explicit')
deleting → refuse when approval_instances references this template with status='pending' (`TEMPLATE_HAS_ACTIVE_INSTANCES`); allow otherwise (soft-delete preserves audit trail)
creating → populate ULID prefix `api_`
creating → snapshot template_version from approval_templates.version (immutable pin — subsequent template edits don't change this instance's semantics)
creating → compute expires_at = requested_at + template.expires_after_hours
creating → REFUSE if tenant hasn't consumed approvals_enabled entitlement (should never happen — Wave 1b ships all-tiers-on)
creating → fire ApprovalRequested AFTER the requirements have been materialised (dispatched afterCommit)

updating → enforce status-transition matrix: pending→(approved | rejected | expired | cancelled); approved→(executed | rejected_via_execution_failure_remediation); rejected/expired/cancelled/executed are TERMINAL — any transition off them fires ApprovalSecurityViolation + refuses
updating → refuse subject_type/subject_id changes post-create (the subject is pinned at instance-time)
updating → refuse context_json changes post-create (context is the snapshot; edits break audit)
updating → refuse approval_template_id/template_version changes post-create (immutable pin)
updating (status → approved) → set decided_at = now(), set final_decider_id = the last decider on the last requirement; fire ApprovalGranted (SYNCHRONOUS dispatch)
updating (status → rejected) → set decided_at = now() + rejected_at = now(), set final_decider_id + rejection_reason; fire ApprovalRejected
updating (status → executed) → set executed_at = now(); fire ApprovalExecuted
updating (status → expired) → set decided_at = now() + rejected_at IS NULL, set expiry-context in metadata; fire ApprovalExpired
updating (status → cancelled) → set cancelled_at = now(); fire ApprovalCancelled
deleting (soft) → allow when status IN terminal states (rejected, expired, cancelled, executed); refuse when status='pending' or 'approved'
creating → populate ULID prefix `apr_`

creating → snapshot selector_expression + quorum_type + quorum_n + sort_order from the parent approval_template_approvers row (frozen for audit — subsequent template edits don't change these snapshots)

creating → invoke ApproverSelectorEngine::resolve() to compute resolved_approvers (delegation-aware); on failure fire ApproverSelectorEvaluationFailed + REFUSE requirement creation (the parent instance is then rolled back and returns 500 approval.selector_evaluation_failed to the caller)
creating → save selector_snapshot_json = compiled AST for audit
creating → set status='pending' if sort_order = MIN(sort_order) across siblings; else 'blocked'
creating → fire ApprovalRequirementResolved

updating (status → satisfied) → set satisfied_at = now(); fire ApprovalRequirementSatisfied; unblock siblings with next-higher sort_order (status blocked → pending); recompute parent instance's aggregate status (if all requirements satisfied → transition instance to approved)
updating (status → failed) → fire ApprovalRequirementFailed; transition parent instance to rejected
updating → refuse changes to selector_expression / selector_snapshot_json / resolved_approvers / quorum_type / quorum_n / sort_order (all immutable post-materialisation)
creating → populate ULID prefix `apd_`

creating → REFUSE if approver_id NOT IN parent requirement.resolved_approvers → fire ApprovalSecurityViolation(violation_type='forged_decision') → refuse the write with 403 approval.caller_not_approver (P0 audit — closes the direct-DB forgery seam)

creating → REFUSE if parent instance.status != 'pending' (already-decided flows can't accept new decisions) → fire ApprovalSecurityViolation(violation_type='quorum_evaluation_bypass') if the caller reached this observer past an accepted decision (indicates a race + policy failure)
creating → REFUSE if parent requirement.status != 'pending' (blocked/satisfied/failed requirements can't accept new decisions)
creating → REFUSE duplicate decisions from the same approver on the same requirement (composite unique enforces at DB; the observer surfaces `APPROVER_ALREADY_DECIDED`)
creating → capture delegated_from_id if the approver was substituted via active RoleDelegation (looked up by ApproverSelectorEngine's substitution index for this instance)
creating → capture request_ip_hash = SHA-256(request()->ip()); never store the plaintext IP
creating → fire ApprovalDecisionRecorded
created (after commit) → QuorumEvaluator::evaluate(requirement) → if quorum reached: transition requirement.status → satisfied; if quorum blocked by reject: transition requirement.status → failed
updating → REFUSE ALL — decisions are write-once → fire ApprovalSecurityViolation(violation_type='direct_db_write') on attempted update
deleting → REFUSE ALL — same reason
